questions.question: Explain why Mean Squared Error (MSE) is not suitable as a cost function for Logistic Regression. (2-3 marks)
expectedPoints: MSE would result in a non-convex cost function for logistic regression.
A non-convex function has many local minima, making it difficult to find the global optimum using gradient descent.
Log Loss (Cross-Entropy) is used instead, as it is convex and ensures convergence to the global minimum.

questions.question: Describe the process of Polynomial Regression. Discuss its advantages, limitations, and how the problem of overfitting can be mitigated. (5-10 marks)
expectedPoints: Process: Creating new features by raising the original features to powers (x², x³, etc.) and then performing Multiple Linear Regression on these new features.
Advantage: Can model complex, non-linear relationships that a simple straight line cannot.
Limitation: Highly prone to overfitting, especially with high polynomial degrees.
Mitigation 1: Use cross-validation to select the optimal polynomial degree.
Mitigation 2: Use regularization techniques (e.g., Ridge, Lasso) that penalize large coefficients.
Mitigation 3: Collect more training data to help the model generalize better.
